text.title: Design Tokens Fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the design-tokens-fundamentals methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Tokens MUST be organised in three tiers: primitive (raw value), semantic (intent name), component (component-specific). Skipping the semantic tier is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without semantic tier, theming requires component rewrites; with it, themes override one layer.

rule.statement: Source format MUST be W3C DTCG JSON (`$value`, `$type`, optional `$description`); other formats are unacceptable as the canonical source.
rule.rationale: DTCG is the agreed cross-tool format; non-DTCG sources block tool interop.

rule.statement: Every color token used in text/background context MUST carry computed `contrast_ratio` against intended pairings; pairs failing thresholds MUST NOT ship.
rule.rationale: Computing at token authoring time prevents downstream WCAG defects; ratios are a property of the token.

rule.statement: Every motion token MUST declare a `reduced-motion` variant; raw motion-only definitions are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vestibular safety + SC 2.3.3; baking the variant prevents per-component oversight.

rule.statement: Spacing/positioning tokens MUST use logical properties (`inline-start`, `block-end`) when RTL is in scope; left/right tokens MUST be marked LTR-only.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded left/right breaks under dir=rtl; logical properties future-proof tokens.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
